pack.icp: Freelance brand designers with 5-25 recurring clients who spend at least 2 hours per month chasing overdue invoices and worry about damaging client relationships.
pack.wtp: Direct WTP evidence exists: a designer said they would pay for reminders that collect invoices without sounding robotic. Adjacent pricing anchors show invoice tools charging $12-$39/mo.
pack.competitor_gap: Invoice trackers like generic billing tools send fixed reminder sequences; the wedge is relationship-aware tone, client history, and designer-specific follow-up templates.
pack.positioning: Tone-aware invoice follow-up for freelance designers who need to collect late payments without making client relationships awkward.
pack.outreach: I saw designers discussing awkward invoice follow-ups and late payments. I am testing tone-aware reminders for freelancers with recurring clients; would 10 minutes of feedback be useful? Reply stop if not relevant.
pack.landing: Collect overdue invoices without sounding robotic. Tone-aware payment reminders for freelance designers with recurring clients.
pack.roadmap: Day 1: interview 5 freelance designers and record current workaround. Day 2: collect pricing anchors and willingness-to-pay language. Day 3: test two landing headlines with 20 prospects. Day 4: send 10 approved interview drafts. Day 5-6: log objections and pricing reactions. Day 7: build only if 3 prospects confirm urgency and 2 accept the price test.
pack.prospect_sources: Specific sources: r/freelance invoice reminder threads, Designer Hangout billing forum, and review-site complaints about generic invoice trackers.
